failed.title: ISSUE_005: Flags Beacon ROFR triggered by change of control
failed.match: PASS if the report identifies that the Beacon Insights operating agreement contains a right of first refusal (ROFR) triggered on a change of control of Prism, that the merger constitutes such a change of control, and that the memorandum fails to analyze the tax consequences of a potential forced sale of the Beacon interest as a separate pre-closing taxable event outside the 338(h)(10) framework. FAIL if the report does not flag the Beacon ROFR and its tax implications.

failed.title: ISSUE_008b: Notes California retroactive C corp tax risk if S election invalid
failed.match: PASS if the report notes the risk that if Prism's S election were found invalid, California could retroactively impose C corporation taxes. FAIL if this specific California retroactive risk is not mentioned.

failed.title: ISSUE_012a: Identifies missing New York state conformity analysis for 338(h)(10)
failed.match: PASS if the report identifies that the memorandum fails to analyze whether New York conforms to the federal Section 338(h)(10) election for S corporations, despite Prism having operations and employees in New York. FAIL if the report does not flag the missing New York state conformity analysis.

failed.title: ISSUE_012b: Identifies missing Virginia state conformity analysis for 338(h)(10)
failed.match: PASS if the report identifies that the memorandum fails to analyze whether Virginia conforms to the federal Section 338(h)(10) election for S corporations, despite Prism having operations and employees in Virginia. FAIL if the report does not flag the missing Virginia state conformity analysis.

turns.text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

## Context management (automatic)

To keep your context small and your runs cheap, the harness automatically
elides the *raw text* of large `read`/`bash` outputs after they have been on
screen for a few turns, replacing each with a short `[context-saver: …]` stub
that names the file or command. The document was fully read when it was
current — nothing was hidden from your initial pass — only the bulk is dropped
from later turns. Two habits keep this lossless:

- As you read a docum

**CALLOWAY, STERN & WHITAKER LLP** *Attorneys at Law*

301 South Tryon Street, Suite 4200 Charlotte, North Carolina 28202 Telephone: (704) 555-3800 \| Facsimile: (704) 555-3801

**PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL --- ATTORNEY-CLIENT COMMUNICATION / ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT** This memorandum is protected by the attorney-client privilege and the work product doctrine. It is intended solely for the use of the addressee. Any unauthorized disclosure, copying, or distribution is strictly prohibited.

**MEMORANDUM**

**TO:** Ridgeline Capital Partners Fund III, LP

> Attention: Fund III Investment Committee

**FROM:** James Calloway, Senior Tax Partner

> Calloway, Stern & Whitaker LLP Assisted by: Sandra Liu, Associate

**DATE:** February 10, 2025

**RE:** Tax Structure Memorandum --- Acquisition of Prism Health Analytics, Inc.

This memorandum sets forth our analysis of the federal and state income tax consequences arising from the proposed acquisition by Ridgeline Capital Partners Fund III, LP (\"Buyer\" or \"Fund III\") of Prism Health Analytics, Inc. (\"Prism\" or the \"Company\"). Our analysis is based upon our review of the Stock Purchase Agreement dated January 28, 2025, the quality of earnings and tax due diligence report prepared by Thornfield Accounting Group, LLP dated January 10, 2025, the capitalization table of Prism as of December 31, 2024, and related organizational and corporate documents provided to us by the parties and their respective advisors. We have assumed the accuracy and completeness of all factual information provided to us without independent verification. This memorandum addresses the federal and applicable state income tax consequences of the proposed transaction structure, including the contemplated election under Section 338(h)(10) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended (the \"Code\"). All references to \"Section\" or \"Treas. Reg.\" are to provisions of the Code and the Treasury Regulations promulgated thereunder, respectively, unless otherwise indicated. Our state tax analysis is focused principally on North Carolina; California entity-level tax considerations are noted briefly.

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[I. Executive Summary]{.underline}**

Ridgeline Capital Partners Fund III, LP (\"Buyer\") proposes to acquire one hundred percent (100%) of the outstanding stock of Prism Health Analytics, Inc. (\"Prism\" or the \"Company\"), a Delaware S corporation, through a reverse triangular merger. Ridgeline Acquisition Sub, Inc. (\"Merger Sub\"), a newly formed Delaware corporation and wholly owned subsidiary of Fund III, will merge with and into Prism, with Prism surviving the merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of Fund III.

The agreed-upon enterprise value for the transaction is \$425,000,000. After deducting approximately \$48,000,000 in funded debt at closing and approximately \$7,000,000 in seller transaction expenses, the equity value payable to the Prism shareholders is \$370,000,000. The parties have executed a Stock Purchase Agreement dated January 28, 2025, with an expected closing date of March 15, 2025.

Critically, the parties intend to make a joint election under Section 338(h)(10) of the Code to treat the stock acquisition as a deemed asset purchase for federal (and applicable state) income tax purposes. Based on our preliminary analysis, the Aggregate Deemed Sale Price (\"ADSP\") is \$473,000,000, and we have prepared a preliminary allocation of the ADSP among the seven asset classes prescribed by the residual method under Section 1060 and Treas. Reg. 1.338-7.

The Section 338(h)(10) election provides two primary benefits in this context. First, it provides Buyer with a stepped-up tax basis in all of Prism\'s assets equal to the allocated ADSP, generating substantial future depreciation and amortization deductions. Second, because Prism is an S corporation, the gain recognized on the deemed asset sale passes through to Prism\'s individual shareholders on their final S corporation K-1 schedules, rather than being subject to double taxation at both the corporate and shareholder level. No entity-level built-in gains tax under Section 1374 will apply because Prism\'s five-year recognition period following its conversion from C corporation status expired on December 31, 2021.

This memorandum is based upon our review of the Stock Purchase Agreement, the Thornfield Accounting Group tax due diligence report, the Company\'s capitalization table and corporate records, and discussions with the parties\' respective advisors. Our state tax analysis addresses North Carolina in detail and notes certain California entity-level tax considerations. We recommend that each shareholder consult with his or her individual tax advisor regarding state-specific filing obligations.

The sections that follow provide a detailed analysis of the transaction structure, the Section 338(h)(10) election mechanics, the purchase price allocation, the tax consequences to the selling shareholders and to Buyer, and the applicable state tax considerations.

**[II. Transaction Overview]{.underline}**

**[II.A. Parties]{.underline}**

The following is a summary of the principal parties to the transaction and the relevant entities, shareholders, and advisors.

**Buyer.** Ridgeline Capital Partners Fund III, LP (\"Fund III\" or \"Buyer\") is a Delaware limited partnership. The general partner of Fund III is Ridgeline Capital GP III, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company. Fund III is a mid-market private equity fund (vintage 2021) with \$1.4 billion in committed capital, focused on investments in the healthcare services and healthcare technology sectors.

**Merger Sub.** Ridgeline Acquisition Sub, Inc. (\"Merger Sub\") is a Delaware corporation newly formed and wholly owned by Fund III. Merger Sub was formed solely for purposes of effecting this acquisition and has no assets, operations, or liabilities other than those incident to its formation and the transactions contemp

… [clipped 53318 bytes]

**[I. Executive Summary]{.underline}**

Thornfield Accounting Group, LLP (\"Thornfield\") was engaged by Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP (\"Ridgeline\"), through its tax counsel Calloway, Stern & Whitaker LLP (\"CSW\"), to conduct tax due diligence on Prism Health Analytics, Inc. (\"Prism\" or the \"Company\"), a Delaware S corporation (EIN 81-2947365) headquartered at 4200 Lake Boone Trail, Suite 500, Raleigh, NC 27607.

**Scope.** Our engagement encompassed a review of federal and state income tax compliance, verification of the Company\'s S corporation election and ongoing eligibility, analysis of tax attributes and carryforwards, examination of intercompany transactions between Prism and its affiliates, review of equity compensation arrangements, and analysis of subsidiary tax classifications.

**Period Reviewed.** Tax years 2017 through 2024 (S corporation years) and the short C corporation year (June 14, 2016 through December 31, 2016).

**Transaction Context.** Ridgeline Capital Partners Fund III, LP proposes to acquire 100% of the outstanding stock of Prism via a reverse triangular merger with Ridgeline Acquisition Sub, Inc. (\"Merger Sub\"). The parties anticipate making a joint election under Section 338(h)(10) of the Internal Revenue Code. The transaction values the enterprise at \$425,000,000 and equity at \$370,000,000. The expected closing

**\"Chandra Employment Agreement\"** means the employment agreement between Dr. Naveen Chandra and the Surviving Corporation, in substantially the form attached hereto as Exhibit D.

**\"Chandra Rollover Agreement\"** means the Rollover Equity Contribution Agreement between Dr. Naveen Chandra and Parent, in substantially the form attached hereto as Exhibit C.

**\"Closing\"** means the closing of the transactions contemplated by this Agreement, as described in Section 3.1.

**\"Closing Date\"** means March 15, 2025, or such other date as may be mutually agreed by the parties.

**\"Company\"** means Prism Health Analytics, Inc., a Delaware S corporation, EIN 81-2947365, with its principal office at 4200 Lake Boone Trail, Suite 500, Raleigh, NC 27607.

**\"Company Subsidiary\"** means each of (a) Prism Data Services, LLC, a North Carolina limited liability company (EIN 86-5512340), which is treated as a disregarded entity for federal income tax purposes and is wholly owned by the Company, and (b) Beacon Insights, LLC (to the extent of the Company\'s 80.5% membership interest therein, it being understood that Beacon Insights is a separate legal entity classified as a partnership for federal income tax purposes).

**\"Enterprise Value\"** means Four Hundred Twenty-Five Million Dollars (\$425,000,000).

**\"Equity Value\"** means Three Hundred Seventy Million Dollars (\$370,000,000), calculated as Enterprise Value minus Funded Indebtedness minus Seller Transaction Expenses (i.e., \$425,000,000 − \$48,000,000 − \$7,000,000 = \$370,000,000).

**\"Escrow Agent\"** means First Atlantic Bank, N.A., 500 West Main Street, Durham, NC 27701.

CONFIDENTIAL --- PROVIDED IN CONNECTION WITH DUE DILIGENCE FOR PROPOSED ACQUISITION OF PRISM HEALTH ANALYTICS, INC.

**AMENDED AND RESTATED OPERATING AGREEMENT**

**OF**

**BEACON INSIGHTS, LLC**

**A North Carolina Limited Liability Company**

*Selected Provisions --- Excerpted for Transaction Review Purposes*

Effective Date: August 1, 2018

Originally formed by filing of Articles of Organization with the North Carolina Secretary of State on March 22, 2018.

Employer Identification Number: 84-3219876

This document contains selected provisions only. Sections not relevant to the transaction review have been omitted and are marked with \"\[Provisions Omitted\]\" where they would otherwise appear.

**[Preamble]{.underline}**

This Amended and Restated Operating Agreement (this \"Agreement\") of Beacon Insights, LLC, a North Carolina limited liability company (the \"Company\"), is entered into effective as of August 1, 2018 (the \"Effective Date\"), by and among the Members listed on Schedule A attached hereto.

**RECITALS**

**WHEREAS**, the Company was organized under the North Carolina Limited Liability Company Act (N.C. Gen. Stat. § 57D-1-01 et seq.) by the filing of Articles of Organization with the North Carolina Secretary of State on March 22, 2018;

**WHEREAS**, the initial members of the Company entered into an Operating Agreement dated as of March 22, 2018 (the \"Original Operating Agreement\"), and the Members now desire to amend and restate the Original Operating Agreement in its entirety as set forth herein;

**WHEREAS**, the Company is engaged in the business of providing data analytics, data engineering, and healthcare data insights services to healthcare providers, payors, and related entities (the \"Business\"); and

**WHEREAS**, the Company intends to be classified as a partnership for federal and state income tax purposes and shall not elect to be treated as a corporation under Treasury Regulation Section 301.7701-3;

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Members agree as follows:

**[Article I --- Definitions]{.underline}**

As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:

**\"Affiliate\"** means, with respect to any Person, any other Person directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by, or under common control with such Person. For purposes of this definition, \"control\" means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise. For the avoidance of doubt, Prism Health Analytics, Inc. and its subsidiaries are Affiliates of the Company.

**\"Capital Account\"** means the capital account maintained for each Member in accordance with Section 704(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended (the \"Code\"), and Treasury Regulation Section 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv).

**\"Change of Control\"** means, with respect to any Member, (a) a merger, consolidation, or other business combination transaction involving such Member as a result of which the equity holders of such Member immediately prior to such transaction hold less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting equity interests of the surviving entity, (b) a sale, transfer, or other disposition of all or substantially all of the assets of such Member, or (c) any other transaction or series of related transactions resulting in a change in the beneficial ownership of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting equity interests of such Member.

**\"Company\"** means Beacon Insights, LLC, a North Carolina limited liability company.

**\"Fair Market Value\"** means the price at which a willing buyer and a willing seller, neither being under any compulsion to buy or sell, and both having reasonable knowledge of relevant facts, would agree to transact, as determined in accordance with Section 8.04 of this Agreement.

**\"Fiscal Year\"** means the calendar year ending December 31.

**\"Intellectual Property\"** or **\"IP\"** means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, software, databases, proprietary data sets, algorithms, and other intangible property.

**\"IP License Agreement\"** means that certain Intellectual Property License Agreement dated August 1, 2018, between Prism Health Analytics, Inc. and the Company, as amended from time to time.

**\"Majority Interest\"** means Members holding more than fifty percent (50%) of the aggregate Percentage Interests.

**\"Management Services Agreement\"** means that certain Management Services Agreement dated August 1, 2018, between Prism Health Analytics, Inc. and the Company, as amended from time to time.

**\"Member\"** means each Person listed on Schedule A hereto, as updated from time to time, who holds a Membership Interest in the Company.

**\"Membership Interest\"** means the entire ownership interest of a Member in the Company, including such Member\'s rights to allocations of profits and losses, rights to distributions, and rights to vote or participate in management as set forth in this Agreement.

**\"Percentage Interest\"** means, with respect to each Member, the percentage set forth opposite such Member\'s name on Schedule A hereto.

**\"Person\"** means any individual, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, trust, estate, or other entity.

**\"ROFR Notice\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 8.03(b).

**\"Transfer\"** means any direct or indirect sale, assignment, pledge, encumbrance, hypothecation, gift, or other disposition of any kind, whether voluntary or involuntary, by operation of law or otherwise.

**\"Unrealized Receivables\"** shall have the meaning set forth in Section 751(c) of the Code.

**[Article II --- Formation and Purpose]{.underline}**

**Section 2.01 --- Formation.*

From: Derek Whitfield, Managing Director, Linden Rock Advisory,
 LLC <dwhitfield@lindenrockadvisory.com>
To: James Calloway, Calloway, Stern & Whitaker LLP <jcalloway@cswlaw.com>
Cc: Sandra Liu, Calloway, Stern & Whitaker LLP <sliu@cswlaw.com>; Tara Brennan,
 Linden Rock Advisory, LLC <tbrennan@lindenrockadvisory.com>
Date:
Subject: QSBS Eligibility — Angel Investor Shareholders / Prism Health
 Analytics Transaction
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable
MIME-Version: 1.0

James,

I hope this note finds you well. I know everyone has been heads-down on dilig=
ence for the Prism Health Analytics, Inc. acquisition by Ridgeline Capital Pa=
rtners, and I appreciate the work your team has been putting in since we sign=
ed the LOI back on September 12. I'm writing today on behalf of several of Pr=
ism's angel investor shareholders who have raised an important tax question t=
hat we believe warrants formal analysis as part of the transaction structure =
work product.

As you know, the 14 individual angel investors and early employees who collec=
tively hold the remaining 20% of Prism common stock will be cashing out entir=
ely in this transaction. A group of these shareholders has asked Linden Rock =
to raise the question of whether their Prism stock qualifies as "qualified sm=
all business stock" under Section 1202 of the Internal Revenue Code =E2=80=94=
 and, critically, whether the QSBS exclusion will be available to them given =
the proposed transaction structure.

Let me lay out the relevant facts and questions as clearly as I can.

**Background:**

Several of the angel investors acquired their Prism common stock in 2016, sho=
rtly after the company was incorporated on June 14, 2016. At that time, Prism=
 was organized as a C corporation. Prism maintained C corporation status from=
 its date of incorporation through December 31, 2016 =E2=80=94 a period of ap=
proximately six and a half months =E2=80=94 before electing S corporation sta=
tus effective January 1, 2017. Prism has been an S corporation continuously s=
ince that date.

The investors understand that Section 1202 provides for up to 100% exclusion =
of gain on the sale of qualified small business stock acquired after Septembe=
r 27, 2010, provided the stock was held for more than five years, was issued =
by a C corporation at the time of issuance, and the issuing corporation met t=
he $50 million aggregate gross assets test at the time of stock issuance. The=
 investors have held their stock for over eight years at this point and belie=
ve the five-year holding period requirement is clearly satisfied, with closin=
g currently anticipated for early 2025.

The financial stakes here are significant. The total consideration allocable =
to the 14 other shareholders is $74,000,000, all cash. Individual shareholder=
s in this group could face gains in the millions of dollars. The QSBS exclusi=
on =E2=80=94 if available =E2=80=94 would be extremely material to their afte=
r-tax proceeds from this transaction.

**Questions for Analysis:**

The shareholders and our team have identified the following specific question=
s that we would like your firm to address:

1. **C-to-S Conversion:** Does the fact that Prism converted from C corporati=
on to S corporation status after only approximately six and a half months (th=
e entirety of 2016) affect QSBS eligibility? Is stock that was issued by a co=
rporation while it was a C corporation, but which subsequently elected S corp=
oration status, still considered "qualified small business stock" within the =
meaning of Section 1202?

2. **Interaction with Section 338(h)(10) Election:** This is the question tha=
t concerns the investors most. The proposed transaction structure contemplate=
s a Section 338(h)(10) election, which as we understand it treats the transac=
tion as a deemed asset sale rather than a stock sale for federal income tax p=
urposes. Section 1202 by its terms applies to gain on the "sale or exchange" =
of "qualified small business stock." If the 338(h)(10) election recharacteriz=
es the transaction as a deemed asset sale, does the QSBS exclusion still appl=
y to the shareholders' gains? Or does the deemed asset sale treatment effecti=
vely eliminate the shareholders' ability to claim the exclusion?

3. **Qualified Small Business Requirements:** Did Prism meet the "qualified s=
mall business" requirements at the time of stock issuance =E2=80=94 specifica=
lly, the $50 million aggregate gross assets test under Section 1202(d)? We be=
lieve the answer is clearly yes given Prism's size in 2016, but we want to co=
nfirm that the analysis has been done.

4. **California Residents:** Two of the 14 shareholders are California reside=
nts. We are aware that California does not conform to the federal Section 120=
2 exclusion, but the investors would like to understand their federal tax pos=
ition clearly, separate and apart from the state-level issue.

James, given the materiality of this issue to the selling shareholders, we ar=
e requesting that Calloway, Stern & Whitaker provide a written analysis of QS=
BS eligibility =E2=80=94 ideally as a dedicated section in the transaction st=
ructure memorandum your team is preparing, or alternatively as a standalone m=
emo. Several of the angel investors are making investment and personal financ=
ial planning decisions based on their understanding of the tax treatment, and=
 they need a definitive answer before they sign the definitive purchase agree=
ment.

I recognize this adds to the workload, and I appreciate your team's willingne=
ss to take it on. This is time-sensitive =E2=80=94 we would like to have the =
analysis in hand before the shareholders are asked to commit to the definitiv=
e documents. If you could aim to have something to us by early December, that=
 would be very helpful.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you need any additional information fro=
m us or from the Prism management

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

turns.result:
# Tax Issue Identification Report

## Review of Transaction Structure Memorandum --- Acquisition of Prism Health Analytics, Inc.

**Prepared by:** Transaction Tax Review **Date of Review:** February 2025 **Subject Memorandum:** "Tax Structure Memorandum --- Acquisition of Prism Health Analytics, Inc.," prepared by Calloway, Stern & Whitaker LLP, dated February 10, 2025 (the "Memo") **Transaction:** Acquisition of Prism Health Analytics, Inc. ("Prism") by Ridgeline Capital Partners Fund III, LP ("Fund III" or "Buyer") via reverse triangular merger with joint Section 338(h)(10) election

## I. Executive Summary

This report identifies issues, inconsistencies, and omissions in the Tax Structure Memorandum prepared by Calloway, Stern & Whitaker LLP ("CSW") by cross-referencing the Memo against six supporting deal documents: the Thornfield Accounting Group tax due diligence report, the Agreement and Plan of Merger (excerpts), the Beacon Insights, LLC operating agreement, the Thornfield asset valuation summary, the Prism capitalization table, and the Linden Rock Advisory QSBS email thread.

**In total, 27 distinct issues were identified**, distributed as follows:

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Severity                Count            Description
  ----------------------- ---------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Critical**            3                Errors or omissions that could invalidate the transaction structure, produce materially incorrect tax results, or constitute a failure to deliver promised analysis

  **High**                7                Material errors or omissions affecting tax consequences to the parties or deal mechanics

  **Moderate**            10               Notable omissions or inconsistencies requiring attention before closing

  **Low**                 7                Minor inconsistencies, documentation gaps, or items requiring verification
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Most Critical Findings

**1. The Aggregate Deemed Sale Price (ADSP) is overstated by \$48 million due to double-counting of funded debt.** The Memo computes ADSP as enterprise value (\$425M) plus funded debt (\$48M) = \$473M. This is mathematically incorrect: enterprise value already embeds the debt component (EV = equity value + net debt). The correct ADSP, per Treas. Reg. §1.338-4 and as independently computed by Thornfield, is approximately \$425M (amount realized on the stock of \$370M + target liabilities of \$48M funded debt + \$7M seller transaction expenses). The \$48M overstatement cascades through the entire analysis --- inflating the deemed sale gain, the Class VII goodwill residual, Buyer's amortization deductions, and every shareholder's tax liability. The Thornfield asset valuation summary explicitly flags this as a "SIGNIFICANT DISCREPANCY."

**2. The Memo contradicts its own cited due diligence source on S corporation shareholder eligibility.** The Memo states that "no events causing a termination of the S election have been identified" and that "we are not aware of any ineligible shareholders." The Thornfield tax due diligence report --- which the Memo expressly relies upon --- flags S corporation eligibility as a **Critical** open item for 2 of 8 restricted stock holders (an H-1B visa holder with unverified substantial-presence-test compliance, and a possible transfer of shares to an ineligible family LLC). The capitalization table independently identifies **three** restricted stock holders with unverified eligibility. If the S election was inadvertently terminated in any prior year, the Section 338(h)(10) election is unavailable, and Prism would be treated as a C corporation with cascading entity-level tax for all open years. The validity of the S election is a threshold requirement for the entire transaction structure.

**3. The QSBS (Section 1202) analysis was explicitly promised in writing but is entirely absent from the Memo.** The Linden Rock email thread documents that CSW committed in November 2024 to include a QSBS analysis in the transaction structure memorandum "well before the purchase agreement is executed." Linden Rock followed up on January 6, 2025, noting that no analysis had been received. The purchase agreement was signed January 28, 2025; the Memo is dated February 10, 2025. The Memo contains no QSBS analysis whatsoever. This omission is material: the founders and at least eight early investors acquired shares during Prism's C corporation period and may be eligible for up to 100% gain exclusion under Section 1202, but the interaction between Section 1202 and the Section 338(h)(10) deemed asset sale framework is a genuine, unresolved legal question that could eliminate the exclusion entirely.

### Cumulative Quantitative Impact of the Critical and High Calculation Errors

The ADSP overstatement (\$48M), the aggregate tax basis understatement (\$21.5M), and the Class V allocation understatement (\$28.5M) combine to produce a materially distorted purchase price allocation and gain computation:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Figure                                   Per Memo      Per Supporting Documents           Variance
  ---------------------------------------- ------------- ---------------------------------- ----------------------
  ADSP                                     \$473.0M      \$425.0M                           +\$48.0M overstated

  Aggregate adjusted tax basis of

… [clipped 1803 bytes]

  **Total**                  **\$430.2M**   **\~\$360.7M**                      **\~\$69.5M**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Note: The corrected gain figures above use the corrected ADSP (\$425M) and the asset valuation summary's aggregate basis (\$64.3M). If the Memo's basis figure (\$42.8M) is used with the corrected ADSP, the total gain would be \~\$382.2M. The exact figure depends on the final reconciled basis.*

## V. Summary of All Findings

  C-3     Critical            QSBS (§1202) analysis --- promised in writing but entirely omitted                         Entire Memo

  H-1     High                Class V allocation understated by \$28.5M (developed technology/software)                  §IV.C

  H-2     High                Beacon IP License Agreement --- automatic termination on change of control not addressed   §VIII

  H-3     High                Beacon ROFR --- triggered by change of control of Prism, not analyzed                      §VIII

  H-4     High                Post-closing entity conversion recommendation legally erroneous (per se corporation)       §VII.B

  H-5     High                Escrow tax treatment --- Memo conflicts with Stock Purchase Agreement                      §V.C

  H-6     High                Aggregate adjusted tax basis inconsistency (\$42.8M vs. \$64.3M)                           §III.B, §V.A

  H-7     High                Rollover equity / §338(h)(10) interaction not analyzed                                     §V.B, §II.B

  M-1     Moderate            AAA / AE&P / deemed liquidation dis

The deliverable is complete and validated. Here's a summary of what I produced.

## Deliverable: `tax-issue-identification-report.docx`

I reviewed the **Tax Structure Memorandum** (Calloway, Stern & Whitaker LLP, dated Feb. 10, 2025) against all six supporting deal documents — the Thornfield tax due diligence report, the Agreement and Plan of Merger excerpts, the Beacon Insights operating agreement, the Thornfield asset valuation summary, the Prism capitalization table, and the Linden Rock QSBS email thread — and produced a severity-ranked issue report with an executive summary.

### What the report contains

- **Executive Summary** with a severity distribution table, the three most critical findings highlighted, a cumulative quantitative impact table, and an overall assessment
- **Scope & Documents Reviewed** section
- **27 findings ranked by severity**, each with memo location, supporting document citation, description, impact, and recommendation:
  - **3 Critical** — ADSP overstated $48M (double-counting funded debt); S-corp shareholder eligibility critical open item ignored/contradicted; QSBS analysis promised in writing but entirely omitted
  - **7 High** — Class V understated $28.5M; Beacon IP license auto-termination on change of control; Beacon ROFR triggered; legally erroneous post-closing check-the-box recommendation; escrow tax treatment conflicting with the purchase agreement; aggregate tax basis inconsistency ($42.8M vs. $64.3M); rollover/338(h)(10) interaction unanalyzed
  - **10 Moderate** — AAA/AE&P omitted; §751 hot assets; §754 election; NC license termination consequences; §482 transfer pricing ($6.3M); suspended R&D credits; shareholder count discrepancies; management fee CPI noncompliance; basic vs. fully diluted allocation; sources & uses understated ~$55M
  - **7 Low** — date/citation errors; unverified EBITDA; CA conformity; NY sales tax; anti-churning; non-compete recharacterization; Chandra basis estimate
- **Reconciliation tables** showing the Memo's figures vs. the correct figures (ADSP, PPA, deemed sale gain, per-shareholder gain)
- **Master summary table** of all 27 findings
- **Conclusion** with 8 prioritized recommended actions

### Key headline finding
The Memo's central calculation — the ADSP — is overstated by **$48 million** because it adds funded debt to enterprise value (which already embeds the debt). Combined with a $21.5M basis understatement and a $28.5M Class V understatement, the Memo overstates the deemed sale gain by **~$69.5M** and goodwill by **$76.5M**, distorting every shareholder's tax liability and Buy
